contentBlocks.original:
अजानन्तो यान्ति क्षयमवशमन्योन्यकलहै-
-रमी मायाग्रन्थौ तव परिलुठन्तः समयिनः ।
जगन्मातर्जन्मज्वरभयतमः कौमुदि वयं
नमस्ते कुर्वाणाः शरणमुपयामो भगवतीम् ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajānantō yānti kṣayamavaśamanyōnyakalahai-
-ramī māyāgranthau tava pariluṭhantaḥ samayinaḥ ।
jaganmātarjanmajvarabhayatamaḥ kaumudi vayaṃ
namastē kurvāṇāḥ śaraṇamupayāmō bhagavatīm ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Not knowing [the truth], these practitioners (samayins) caught in the knot of your Māyā rush helplessly to destruction through mutual conflicts. O Mother of the World, moonlight that dispels the darkness of the fever and fear of birth — making salutation to you, we take refuge in Bhagavatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: ajānantaḥ — not knowing; yānti kṣayam — rush to destruction; avaśam — helplessly; anyōnya-kalahaiḥ — through mutual conflicts; amī māyā-granthau tava pariluṭhantaḥ — these ones rolling/caught in the knot of your Māyā; samayinaḥ — practitioners of the samaya tradition; jagat-mātar — O Mother of the World; janma-jvara-bhaya-tamaḥ — darkness of the fear and fever of birth (saṃsāra); kaumudi — moonlight; vayaṃ namastē kurvāṇāḥ — we, making salutation to you; śaraṇam upayāmaḥ — we take refuge; bhagavatīm — in Bhagavatī.

contentBlocks.original:
वचस्तर्कागम्यस्वरसपरमानन्दविभव-
-प्रबोधाकाराय द्युतितुलितनीलोत्पलरुचे ।
शिवाद्याराध्याय स्तनभरविनम्राय सततं
नमस्तस्मै कस्मैचन भवतु मुग्धाय महसे ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vachastarkāgamyasvarasaparamānandavibhava-
-prabōdhākārāya dyutitulitanīlōtpalaruchē ।
śivādyārādhyāya stanabharavinamrāya satataṃ
namastasmai kasmaichana bhavatu mugdhāya mahasē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: To that radiance — inaccessible to speech and logic, its essence the supreme bliss, taking the form of awakening, of brilliance rivaling the blue lotus — ever worshipped by Śiva and others, ever bowing under the weight of the breasts — to that certain mysterious luminosity, let there be salutation.
contentBlocks.meanings: vacas-tarka-agamya — inaccessible to speech and logic; svarasa-parama-ānanda-vibhava-prabōdha-ākārāya — whose form is the awakening that is the essence of supreme bliss; dyuti-tulita-nīla-utpala-ruchē — of brilliance rivaling the blue water-lily/lotus; śiva-ādi-ārādhyāya — worshipped by Śiva and others; stana-bhara-vinamrāya — ever bowing under the weight of the breasts; satatam namastasmai kasmaichana bhavatu mugdhāya mahasē — to that certain bewildering/enchanting radiance, let there be salutation.

contentBlocks.original:
अनाद्यन्ताभेदप्रणयरसिकापि प्रणयिनी
शिवस्यासीर्यत्त्वं परिणयविधौ देवि गृहिणी ।
सवित्री भूतानामपि यदुदभूः शैलतनया
तदेतत्संसारप्रणयनमहानाटकमुखम् ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anādyantābhēdapraṇayarasikāpi praṇayinī
śivasyāsīryattvaṃ pariṇayavidhau dēvi gṛhiṇī ।
savitrī bhūtānāmapi yadudabhūḥ śailatanayā
tadētatsaṃsārapraṇayanamahānāṭakamukham ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Though eternally relishing love with Śiva in non-dual union (anādi-anta-abhēda), yet O Devī, you became his spouse in the wedding ceremony. Though the generatrix (sāvitrī) of all beings, you took birth as the daughter of the mountain-king. This — O how wondrous — is the opening act of the grand drama of worldly creation.
contentBlocks.meanings: anādi-anta-abhēda-praṇaya-rasikā api — though eternally relishing the love of beginningless, endless non-duality; praṇayinī — yet a lover (taking the role); śivasya āsīḥ — you became [Śiva's]; pariṇaya-vidhau gṛhiṇī — a housewife in the wedding ceremony; dēvi — O Goddess; savitrī bhūtānām — generatrix of all beings; api — even though; yat udabhūḥ śaila-tanayā — you were born as the mountain's daughter; tat etat — this alone is; saṃsāra-praṇayana — the creation/setting-in-motion of the world; mahā-nāṭaka-mukham — the opening act of the great drama.

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रुवन्त्येके तत्त्वं भगवति सदन्ये विदुरस-
-त्परे मातः प्राहुस्तव सदसदन्ये सुकवयः ।
परे नैतत्सर्वं समभिदधते देवि सुधिय-
-स्तदेतत्त्वन्मायाविलसितमशेषं ननु शिवे ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bruvantyēkē tattvaṃ bhagavati sadanyē vidurasa-
-tparē mātaḥ prāhustava sadasadanyē sukavayaḥ ।
parē naitatsarvaṃ samabhidadhatē dēvi sudhiya-
-stadētattvanmāyāvilasitamaśēṣaṃ nanu śivē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Some call your nature Sat (pure Being), O Bhagavatī; others understand it as Asat (non-being); yet other fine poets declare it as Sat-asat (both being and non-being); still others call it Transcendent (Para). But the truly discerning ones, O Devī, declare none of these fully grasps [you] — all of it, O Śivā, is your Māyā's play.
contentBlocks.meanings: bruvanti ēkē tattvaṃ — some declare [your] tattva/nature; bhagavati sat — O Bhagavatī, as Sat (pure Being); anyē viduḥ asat — others know it as Asat (non-being); parē mātaḥ prāhuḥ tava sadasat — others, O Mother, declare it as Sat-asat; anyē su-kavayaḥ paraṃ — other fine poets [call it] Para (transcendent); parē naitad sarvaṃ samabhidadhatē — but others declare none of these apply; dēvi su-dhiyaḥ — O Goddess, the truly wise; tat etat tvan-māyā-vilasitam aśēṣaṃ — all of this is entirely your Māyā's play; nanu śivē — indeed, O auspicious one.

contentBlocks.original:
लुठद्गुञ्जाहारस्तनभरनमन्मध्यलतिका-
-मुदञ्चद्धर्माम्भः कणगुणितवक्त्राम्बुजरुचम् ।
शिवं पार्थत्राणप्रवणमृगयाकारगुणितं
शिवामन्वग्यान्तीं शरणमहमन्वेमि शबरीम् ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
luṭhadguñjāhārastanabharanamanmadhyalatikā-
-mudañchaddharmāmbhaḥ kaṇaguṇitavaktrāmbujarucham ।
śivaṃ pārthatrāṇapravaṇamṛgayākāraguṇitaṃ
śivāmanvagyāntīṃ śaraṇamahamanvēmi śabarīm ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With rolling guñjā-berry necklace, with waist-stalk bending under the breast's weight, with face-lotus brightened by drops of dharma-water — I take refuge in Śabarī, who follows Śivā [the goddess], herself following Śiva [the hunter-god] engaged in the hunt of saving Arjuna.
contentBlocks.meanings: luṭhat-guñjā-hāra — with rolling/tumbling guñjā-berry necklace; stana-bhara-naman-madhya-latikā — with waist-creeper bowing under breast-weight; udañchat-dharma-ambhaḥ-kaṇa-guṇita-vaktra-ambuja-rucham — face-lotus brightened by drops of dharma-water (tears of devotion); śivaṃ pārtha-trāṇa-praṇava-mṛgayā-kāra-guṇitaṃ — Śiva [the hunter] intent on the hunt of saving Arjuna (Pārtha); śivāṃ anvak-yāntīṃ — following Śivā (the Goddess-form); śaraṇam aham anvēmi śabarīm — I take refuge in Śabarī.

contentBlocks.original:
मिथः केशाकेशिप्रथननिधनास्तर्कघटनाः
बहुश्रद्धाभक्तिप्रणतिविषयाः शास्त्रविधयः ।
प्रसीद प्रत्यक्षीभव गिरिसुते देहि शरणं
निरालम्बं चेतः परिलुठति पारिप्लवमिदम् ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mithaḥ kēśākēśiprathananidhanāstarkaghaṭanāḥ
bahuśraddhābhaktipraṇativiṣayāḥ śāstravidhayaḥ ।
prasīda pratyakṣībhava girisutē dēhi śaraṇaṃ
nirālambaṃ chētaḥ pariluṭhati pāriplavamidam ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The philosophical arguments that end in mutual hair-pulling, the constructions of logic, the scriptural procedures that are objects of faith, devotion, and obeisance — O please relent, O daughter of the mountain, appear directly, grant refuge! This supportless, unsteady mind rolls about, helpless and adrift.
contentBlocks.meanings: mithaḥ kēśākēśi-prathana-nidhanāḥ — ending in mutual hair-pulling and destruction; tarka-ghaṭanāḥ — constructions/exercises of logic; bahu-śraddhā-bhakti-praṇati-viṣayāḥ — objects of much faith, devotion, and obeisance; śāstra-vidhayaḥ — scriptural procedures; prasīda — O please relent/be gracious; pratyakṣī-bhava — appear directly; giri-sutē — O daughter of the mountain; dēhi śaraṇaṃ — grant refuge; nirālambaṃ chētaḥ — this supportless mind; pariluṭhati — rolls/tosses about; pāriplavam idam — this one that is adrift/vacillating.

contentBlocks.original:
शुनां वा वह्नेर्वा खगपरिषदो वा यदशनं
कदा केन क्वेति क्वचिदपि न कश्चित्कलयति ।
अमुष्मिन्विश्वासं विजहिहि ममाह्नाय वपुषि
प्रपद्येथाश्चेतः सकलजननीमेव शरणम् ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śunāṃ vā vahnērvā khagapariṣadō vā yadaśanaṃ
kadā kēna kvēti kvachidapi na kaśchitkalayati ।
amuṣminviśvāsaṃ vijahihi mamāhnāya vapuṣi
prapadyēthāśchētaḥ sakalajananīmēva śaraṇam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who will know where, by whom, when — what a dog eats, what fire [consumes], what a flock of birds [devours]? No one anywhere knows this. O mind, abandon trust in this perishable body of mine — surrender to the Sakala-Jananī alone as your refuge.
contentBlocks.meanings: śunāṃ vā vahnēḥ vā khaga-pariṣadaḥ vā yat aśanaṃ — what a dog, fire, or flock of birds eats; kadā kēna kva iti — where, by whom, when; kvachid api na kaśchit kalayati — no one anywhere perceives this; amuṣmin viśvāsaṃ vijahihi — abandon trust in this [body/world]; mama āhnāya vapuṣi — in this perishable body of mine; prapadyēthāḥ chētaḥ — O mind, surrender; sakala-jananīm ēva śaraṇam — to the Sakala-Jananī (Universal Mother) alone as refuge.

contentBlocks.original:
तटित्कोटिज्योतिर्द्युतिदलितषड्ग्रन्थिगहनं
प्रविष्टं स्वाधारं पुनरपि सुधावृष्टिवपुषा ।
किमप्यष्टाविंशत्किरणसकलीभूतमनिशं
भजे धाम श्यामं कुचभरनतं बर्बरकचम् ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṭitkōṭijyōtirdyutidalitaṣaḍgranthigahanaṃ
praviṣṭaṃ svādhāraṃ punarapi sudhāvṛṣṭivapuṣā ।
kimapyaṣṭāviṃśatkiraṇasakalībhūtamaniśaṃ
bhajē dhāma śyāmaṃ kuchabharanataṃ barbarakacham ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The luminosity of crores of lightning-bolts, whose splendor has broken open the thicket of the six knots — having entered the mūlādhāra, then again taking a form that rains nectar — some radiance ever made whole by twenty-eight rays, dark-hued, bowing under the breast's weight, with disheveled tresses — I adore this.
contentBlocks.meanings: taṭit-kōṭi-jyōtiḥ — the light of crores of lightning; dyuti-dalita-ṣaḍ-granthi-gahanaṃ — whose radiance has broken through the dense thicket of the six knots (chakra-granthis); praviṣṭaṃ svādhāraṃ — having entered the svādhāra (mūlādhāra chakra); punar api sudhā-vṛṣṭi-vapuṣā — and again taking a form of raining nectar; kim api aṣṭa-viṃśat-kiraṇa-sakalī-bhūtam — some [thing] ever made whole by twenty-eight rays (16 lunar kalās + 12 solar?); aniśaṃ bhajē dhāma śyāmaṃ — I always adore this dark-hued radiance; kucha-bhara-nataṃ — bowing under the breast-weight; barbara-kacham — with disheveled hair.

contentBlocks.original:
चतुष्पत्रान्तः षड्दलपुटभगान्तस्त्रिवलय-
-स्फुरद्विद्युद्वह्निद्युमणिनियुताभद्युतिलते ।
षडश्रं भित्त्वादौ दशदलमथ द्वादशदलं
कलाश्रं च द्व्यश्रं गतवति नमस्ते गिरिसुते ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chatuṣpatrāntaḥ ṣaḍdalapuṭabhagāntastrivalaya-
-sphuradvidyudvahnidyumaṇiniyutābhadyutilatē ।
ṣaḍaśraṃ bhittvādau daśadalamatha dvādaśadalaṃ
kalāśraṃ cha dvyaśraṃ gatavati namastē girisutē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having passed through the interior of the four-petaled [mūlādhāra], then through the six-petaled void [svādhiṣṭhāna], through the three-coiled loop, flashing with the brilliance of ten thousand lightning-fires and suns — then piercing the six-angled [maṇipūra], the ten-petaled, the twelve-petaled, the sixteen-petaled triangle, and the bindu — O daughter of the mountain, salutation to you who have traversed all these.
contentBlocks.meanings: chatuṣ-patra-antaḥ — within/beyond the four-petaled [mūlādhāra]; ṣaḍ-dala-puṭa-bhaga-antaḥ — through the cavity of the six-petaled [svādhiṣṭhāna]; tri-valaya — three-coiled (the three coils of the Kuṇḍalinī serpent); sphurat-vidyut-vahni-dyu-maṇi-niyuta-ābha-dyuti-latē — flashing with the brilliance of ten thousand lightning/fire/solar-gems; ṣaḍ-aśraṃ bhitvā ādau — first piercing the six-angled [maṇipūra/triangle]; daśa-dalam — the ten-petaled [maṇipūra or anāhata]; atha dvādaśa-dalam — then the twelve-petaled [anāhata]; kalāśraṃ cha dvyaśraṃ — then the triangle of kalā, and the bindu; gatavati namastē giri-sutē — salutation to you who have gone through all these.

contentBlocks.original:
कुलं केचित्प्राहुर्वपुरकुलमन्ये तव बुधाः
परे तत्सम्भेदं समभिदधते कौलमपरे ।
चतुर्णामप्येषामुपरि किमपि प्राहुरपरे
महामाये तत्त्वं तव कथममी निश्चिनुमहे ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kulaṃ kēchitprāhurvapurakulamanyē tava budhāḥ
parē tatsambhēdaṃ samabhidadhatē kaulamaparē ।
chaturṇāmapyēṣāmupari kimapi prāhuraparē
mahāmāyē tattvaṃ tava kathamamī niśchinumahē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Some call your [nature] 'kula' (tradition/body), O Mahāmāyā; others call it 'akula' (beyond tradition); yet others describe the merging of both as 'kaula'; and still others declare something beyond all four. O Great Illusionist, how shall we, these [finite beings], determine your true tattva?
contentBlocks.meanings: kulaṃ kēchit prāhuḥ — some call [your nature] kula; vapuḥ akula — [others say] akula as [your] body; anyē tava budhāḥ — other wise ones; parē tat-sambhēdaṃ samabhidadhatē — others describe the interpenetration/merging of these; kaulam aparē — others [call it] kaula; chaturṇām api ēṣām upari kim api prāhuḥ aparē — others declare something even beyond these four; mahāmāyē — O great Māyā; tattvaṃ tava katham amī niśchinumahē — how shall we determine your tattva/essence?

contentBlocks.original:
षडध्वारण्यानीं प्रलयरविकोटिप्रतिरुचा
रुचा भस्मीकृत्य स्वपदकमलप्रह्वशिरसाम् ।
वितन्वानः शैवं किमपि वपुरिन्दीवररुचिः
कुचाभ्यामानम्रस्तव पुरुषकारो विजयते ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṣaḍadhvāraṇyānīṃ pralayaravikōṭipratiruchā
ruchā bhasmīkṛtya svapadakamalaprahvaśirasām ।
vitanvānaḥ śaivaṃ kimapi vapurindīvararuchiḥ
kuchābhyāmānamrastava puruṣakārō vijayatē ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who, having incinerated with the radiance of crores of dissolution-suns the forest of the six paths (ṣaḍadhvā), bending forward by the two breasts — that one who manifests a certain Śaiva form, blue as a blue lotus — your advocate (puruṣakāra), O [Goddess], is victorious.
contentBlocks.meanings: ṣaḍ-adhvā-araṇyānīṃ — the forest of the six paths (mantra, pada, varṇa, kalā, tattva, bhuvana); pralaya-ravi-kōṭi-pratirucha — with the radiance rivaling crores of dissolution-suns; ruchā bhasmī-kṛtya — having incinerated with [his] radiance; sva-pada-kamala-prahva-śirasām — [for those who have] bowed heads at his lotus-feet; vitanvānaḥ śaivaṃ kim api vapuḥ — manifesting a certain Śaiva form; indīvara-ruchiḥ — blue as a blue lotus; kuchābhyāṃ ānamraḥ — bowing by the twin breasts; tava puruṣakāraḥ vijayatē — your advocate/champion is victorious.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रकाशानन्दाभ्यामविदितचरीं मध्यपदवीं
प्रविश्यैतद्द्वन्द्वं रविशशिसमाख्यं कबलयन् ।
प्रपद्योर्ध्वं नादं लयदहनभस्मीकृतकुलः
प्रसादात्ते जन्तुः शिवमकुलमम्ब प्रविशति ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prakāśānandābhyāmaviditacharīṃ madhyapadavīṃ
praviśyaitaddvandvaṃ raviśaśisamākhyaṃ kabalayan ।
prapadyōrdhvaṃ nādaṃ layadahanabhasmīkṛtakulaḥ
prasādāttē jantuḥ śivamakulamamba praviśati ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having entered the middle path — unrecognized by [the duality of] Prakāśa and Ānanda — having absorbed this pair called Sun-and-Moon, ascending to the Nāda above, the seeker whose lineage (kula) has been incinerated by the fire of laya — by your grace, O Mother, that creature attains the auspicious, kula-transcendent (akula) Śiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: prakāśa-ānanda-ābhyām aviditacharīṃ — unrecognized/untrodden by Prakāśa and Ānanda (the two aspects of Śiva-consciousness); madhya-padavīṃ — the middle path (suṣumnā); praviśya — having entered; etad dvandvaṃ ravi-śaśi-samākhyaṃ kabalayan — having swallowed this pair called sun (prāṇa/iḍā) and moon (apāna/piṅgalā); prapadya ūrdhvaṃ nādaṃ — ascending to the Nāda above; laya-dahana-bhasmī-kṛta-kulaḥ — whose kula has been incinerated by the fire of laya; prasādāt tē jantuḥ — by your grace, the creature; śivam akulam amba praviśati — enters the auspicious, akula Śiva.

contentBlocks.original:
मनुष्यास्तिर्यञ्चो मरुत इति लोकत्रयमिदं
भवाम्भोधौ मग्नं त्रिगुणलहरीकोटिलुठितम् ।
कटाक्षश्चेद्यत्र क्वचन तव मातः करुणया
शरीरी सद्योऽयं व्रजति परमानन्दतनुताम् ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manuṣyāstiryañchō maruta iti lōkatrayamidaṃ
bhavāmbhōdhau magnaṃ triguṇalaharīkōṭiluṭhitam ।
kaṭākṣaśchēdyatra kvachana tava mātaḥ karuṇayā
śarīrī sadyō'yaṃ vrajati paramānandatanutām ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Humans, animals, celestial beings — this triple world drowning in the ocean of saṃsāra, buffeted by crores of tri-guṇa waves — wherever your compassionate sidelong glance falls upon some being, O Mother, that embodied one immediately attains the state of the body of supreme bliss.
contentBlocks.meanings: manuṣyāḥ tiryañchaḥ marutaḥ iti — humans, animals, and wind-beings (celestials); lōka-trayam idam — this triple world; bhava-ambhōdhau magnaṃ — drowning in the ocean of saṃsāra; tri-guṇa-laharī-kōṭi-luṭhitam — buffeted by crores of tri-guṇa waves; kaṭākṣaḥ chēt — if [your] sidelong glance; yatra kvachana tava mātaḥ karuṇayā — wherever, O Mother, with compassion; śarīrī sadyaḥ ayaṃ — this embodied one immediately; vrajati — attains; paramānanda-tanutām — the state of the body of supreme bliss.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रियङ्गुश्यामाङ्गीमरुणतरवासं किसलयां
समुन्मीलन्मुक्ताफलवहलनेपथ्यसुभगाम् ।
स्तनद्वन्द्वस्फारस्तबकनमितां कल्पलतिकां
सकृद्ध्यायन्तस्त्वां दधति शिवचिन्तामणिपदम् ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
priyaṅguśyāmāṅgīmaruṇataravāsaṃ kisalayāṃ
samunmīlanmuktāphalavahalanēpathyasubhagām ।
stanadvandvasphārastabakanamitāṃ kalpalatikāṃ
sakṛddhyāyantastvāṃ dadhati śivachintāmaṇipadam ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dark-limbed as the priyangu creeper, wearing a garment reddish as tender shoots, adorned with beautiful pearl ornaments in radiant array — she who bows forward under the weight of twin breasts like full clusters: those who even once meditate on you, O wish-creeper, attain the state of Śiva's divine wish-gem (chintāmaṇi-pada).
contentBlocks.meanings: priyangu-śyāma-aṅgīm — dark-limbed as the priyangu (millet/creeper); aruṇa-tara-vāsaṃ — wearing a garment reddish/more-red than tender leaves; kisalayāṃ — tender shoot (young, fresh); samunmīlan-muktā-phala-vahala-nēpathya-subhagām — adorned with beautiful necklaces of full radiant pearls; stana-dvandva-sphāra-stabaka-namitāṃ — bowing under the weight of the twin breast-clusters; kalpa-latikāṃ — wish-creeper; sakṛd dhyāyantaḥ tvāṃ dadhati — those who even once meditate on you, they hold to/attain; śiva-chintāmaṇi-padam — the state of Śiva's chintāmaṇi (divine wish-gem).

contentBlocks.original:
षडाधारावर्तैरपरिमितमन्त्रोर्मिपटलैः
लसन्मुद्राफेनैर्बहुविधलसद्दैवतझषैः ।
क्रमस्रोतोभिस्त्वं वहसि परनादामृतनदी
भवानि प्रत्यग्रा शिवचिदमृताब्धिप्रणयिनी ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṣaḍādhārāvartairaparimitamantrōrmipaṭalaiḥ
lasanmudrāphēnairbahuvidhalasaddaivatajhaṣaiḥ ।
kramasrōtōbhistvaṃ vahasi paranādāmṛtanadī
bhavāni pratyagrā śivachidamṛtābdhipraṇayinī ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With the whirlpools of the six centers, with unlimited layers of mantra-waves, with gleaming mudrā-foam, with deities shining in many forms as fish — you flow as the river of supreme Nāda-nectar, O Bhavānī, ever fresh, one who yearns for the ocean of Śiva-consciousness-nectar.
contentBlocks.meanings: ṣaḍ-ādhāra-āvartaiḥ — with the whirlpools of the six centers (chakras); aparimita-mantra-ūrmi-paṭalaiḥ — with unlimited layers of mantra-waves; lasat-mudrā-phēnaiḥ — with gleaming mudrā-foam; bahu-vidha-lasat-daivata-jhaṣaiḥ — with many kinds of shining divine fish; krama-srōtōbhiḥ — with sequential currents (the krama-tradition?); tvaṃ vahasi — you flow; para-nāda-amṛta-nadī — [as] the river of supreme Nāda-nectar; bhavāni — O Bhavānī; pratyagrā — ever fresh/immediate; śiva-chid-amṛta-abdhi-praṇayinī — yearning for/flowing toward the ocean of Śiva-consciousness-nectar.

contentBlocks.original:
महीपाथोवह्निश्वसनवियदात्मेन्दुरविभि-
-र्वपुर्भिग्रस्ताशैरपि तव कियानम्ब महिमा ।
अमून्यालोक्यन्ते भगवति न कुत्राप्यणुतमा-
-मवस्थां प्राप्तानि त्वयि तु परमव्योमवपुषि ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahīpāthōvahniśvasanaviyadātmēnduravibhi-
-rvapurbhigrastāśairapi tava kiyānamba mahimā ।
amūnyālōkyantē bhagavati na kutrāpyaṇutamā-
-mavasthāṃ prāptāni tvayi tu paramavyōmavapuṣi ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Though [all] your forms are earth, water, fire, wind, ether, soul, moon, and sun — yet how great, O Mother, is your majesty [beyond them]! These are seen everywhere not even attaining their most minute state — but in you, who are the body of the supreme ether (paramavyōma), they find no place at all.
contentBlocks.meanings: mahī-pāthaḥ-vahni-śvasana-viyad-ātma-indu-ravi-bhiḥ vapurbhiḥ — with the forms of earth, water, fire, wind, sky, soul, moon, sun; grastāśaiḥ api — though [these are] swallowing up the directions; tava kiyān amba mahimā — how great is your majesty, O Mother [beyond these]; amūni ālōkyantē bhagavati na kutrāpi aṇu-tamām avasthāṃ prāptāni — these are seen nowhere even attaining their most minute form; tvayi tu parama-vyōma-vapuṣi — but in you, whose body is the supreme ether.

contentBlocks.original:
कलामाज्ञां प्रज्ञां समयमनुभूतिं समरसं
गुरुं पारम्पर्यं विनयमुपदेशं शिवपदम् ।
प्रमाणं निर्वाणं प्रकृतिमभिभूतिं परगुहां
विधिं विद्यामाहुः सकलजननीमेव मुनयः ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalāmājñāṃ prajñāṃ samayamanubhūtiṃ samarasaṃ
guruṃ pāramparyaṃ vinayamupadēśaṃ śivapadam ।
pramāṇaṃ nirvāṇaṃ prakṛtimabhibhūtiṃ paraguhāṃ
vidhiṃ vidyāmāhuḥ sakalajananīmēva munayaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kalā (divine art), Ājñā (command), Prajñā (wisdom), Samaya (mystic union), Anubhūti (experience), Samarasa (equal-taste/equanimity), Guru, Paramparā (lineage), Vinaya (discipline), Upadeśa (initiation-teaching), Śivapada (Śiva's state), Pramāṇa (valid knowledge), Nirvāṇa, Prakṛti (nature), Abhibhūti (transcendence), Paraguhā (the Supreme Cave/Heart) — the sages declare all these to be the Sakala-Jananī alone.
contentBlocks.meanings: kalām — kalā (art, digit of the moon, creative power); ājñāṃ — ājñā (command, the sixth chakra); prajñāṃ — wisdom; samayam — samaya (the Śrī Vidyā esoteric tradition); anubhūtiṃ — direct experience; samarasaṃ — equal-taste (non-dual awareness); guruṃ — the teacher; pāramparyaṃ — lineage; vinayam — discipline; upadeśaṃ — initiation-teaching; śivapadam — the state/feet of Śiva; pramāṇaṃ — valid means of knowledge; nirvāṇaṃ — liberation; prakṛtiṃ — primordial nature; abhibhūtiṃ — transcendence; paraguhāṃ — the Supreme Cave; vidhiṃ — procedure; vidyām — knowledge; āhuḥ sakalajananīm ēva munayaḥ — the sages declare to be the Sakala-Jananī alone.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रलीने शब्दौघे तदनु विरते बिन्दुविभवे
ततस्तत्त्वे चाष्टध्वनिभिरनपायिन्यधिगते ।
श्रिते शाक्ते पर्वण्यनुकलितचिन्मात्र गहनां
स्वसंवित्तिं योगी रसयति शिवाख्यां भगवतीम् ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pralīnē śabdaughē tadanu viratē binduvibhavē
tatastattvē chāṣṭadhvanibhiranapāyinyadhigatē ।
śritē śāktē parvaṇyanukalitachinmātra gahanāṃ
svasaṃvittiṃ yōgī rasayati śivākhyāṃ bhagavatīm ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When the mass of speech dissolves, then when the bindu-manifestation ceases, then when the tattva is reached in its eight indestructible sound-forms — resting in the Śākta culmination, the yogi savors the depth that is pure consciousness alone — that Bhagavatī named Śivā.
contentBlocks.meanings: pralīnē śabda-aughē — when the mass/flood of speech is dissolved; tad-anu viratē bindu-vibhavē — then when the bindu-glory ceases; tatas tattvasya ca aṣṭa-dhvanibhiḥ anapāyinī adhigatē — then when the tattva is reached by/through the eight indestructible sounds; śritē śāktē parvaṇi — resting in the Śākta culmination/festival; anukalita-chit-mātra-gahanāṃ — savoring the depth of pure consciousness alone; sva-saṃvittiṃ yōgī rasayati — the yogi tastes/savors his own self-awareness; śivākhyāṃ bhagavatīm — Bhagavatī known as Śivā.

contentBlocks.original:
परानन्दाकारां निरवधिशिवैश्वर्यवपुषं
निराकारां ज्ञानप्रकृतिमपरिच्छिन्नकरुणाम् ।
सवित्रीं लोकानां निरतिशयधामास्पदपदां
भवो वा मोक्षो वा भवतु भवतीमेव भजताम् ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parānandākārāṃ niravadhiśivaiśvaryavapuṣaṃ
nirākārāṃ jñānaprakṛtimaparichChinnakaruṇām ।
savitrīṃ lōkānāṃ niratiśayadhāmāspadapadāṃ
bhavō vā mōkṣō vā bhavatu bhavatīmēva bhajatām ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of the form of supreme bliss, the body of infinite Śiva-lordship, formless, whose nature is pure knowledge, of boundless compassion, generator (sāvitrī) of all worlds, the abode of the supreme unrivaled light — for those who worship her, let it be saṃsāra or liberation — she is Bhagavatī alone.
contentBlocks.meanings: parānanda-ākārāṃ — of the form of supreme bliss; niravadhi-śivaiśvarya-vapuṣaṃ — whose body is the infinite lordship of Śiva; nirākārāṃ — formless; jñāna-prakṛtiṃ — whose nature is knowledge; aparicchinnakaruṇāṃ — of boundless compassion; savitrīṃ lōkānāṃ — generator of all worlds; niratiśaya-dhāma-āspada-padāṃ — whose place is the abode of the supreme, unsurpassed light; bhavaḥ vā mōkṣaḥ vā bhavatu — let it be saṃsāra or liberation; bhavatīm ēva bhajatām — for those who worship her, she is [all].

contentBlocks.original:
जगत्काये कृत्वा तदपि हृदये तच्च पुरुषे
पुमांसं बिन्दुस्थं तदपि वियदाख्ये च गहने ।
तदेतद्ज्ञानाख्ये तदपि परमानन्दगहने
महाव्योमाकारे त्वदनुभवशीलो विजयते ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagatkāyē kṛtvā tadapi hṛdayē tachcha puruṣē
pumāṃsaṃ bindusthaṃ tadapi viyadākhyē cha gahanē ।
tadētadjñānākhyē tadapi paramānandagahanē
mahāvyōmākārē tvadanubhavaśīlō vijayatē ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having made the universe into a body, and even that into the heart, and the heart into the cosmic Person, and the Person as dwelling in the bindu, and even that in the depth called Ākāśa (sky-consciousness), and even that in what is called Jñāna (knowledge), and even that in the depth of supreme bliss — dwelling in the great ether-form (mahāvyōma): one practiced in the experience of you is victorious.
contentBlocks.meanings: jagat-kāyē kṛtvā — having made the world into a body; tad api hṛdayē — and even that into the heart; tac cha puruṣē — and that into the cosmic Person (puruṣa); pumāṃsaṃ bindu-sthaṃ — the Person dwelling in the bindu; tad api viyadākhyē cha gahanē — and even that in the depth called Viyad/Ākāśa; tad ētat jñānākhyē — and even that in what is called Jñāna; tad api paramānanda-gahanē — and even that in the depth of supreme bliss; mahāvyōma-ākārē — in the form of the great ether; tvad-anubhava-śīlaḥ vijayatē — one practiced/skilled in experiencing you is victorious.

contentBlocks.original:
विधे वेद्ये विद्ये विविधसमये वेदगुलिके
विचित्रे विश्वाद्ये विनयसुलभे वेदजननि ।
शिवज्ञे शूलस्थे शिवपदवदान्ये शिवनिधे
शिवे मातर्मह्यं त्वयि वितर भक्तिं निरुपमाम् ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidhē vēdyē vidyē vividhasamayē vēdagulikē
vichitrē viśvādyē vinayasulabhē vēdajanani ।
śivajñē śūlasthē śivapadavadānyē śivanidhē
śivē mātarmahyaṃ tvayi vitara bhaktiṃ nirupamām ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O You who are the rule of creation (vidhē), the knowable (vēdyē), knowledge (vidyē), the manifold traditions (vividha-samayē), the secret pill of the Vedas (vēda-gulikē), the wondrous (vicitrē), the primordial of the universe (viśvādyē), easily reached by the humble (vinaya-sulabhē), O mother of the Vedas — O knower of Śiva, dweller in the trident, generous bestower of Śiva's state, O treasury of Śiva, O auspicious Śivā — O Mother, bestow upon me unsurpassed devotion to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: vidhē — O one who is the rule/orderer; vēdyē — O knowable; vidyē — O knowledge; vividha-samayē — O manifold tradition; vēda-gulikē — O secret pill of the Vedas; vicitrē — O wondrous one; viśvādyē — O primordial of the world; vinaya-sulabhē — O easily reached through humility; vēdajanani — O mother of the Vedas; śivajñē — O knower of Śiva; śūlasthē — O dweller in the trident (Śiva's triśūla); śiva-pada-vadānyē — O generous giver of Śiva's state; śiva-nidhē — O treasury of Śiva; śivē — O auspicious Śivā; mātar mahyaṃ tvayi vitara bhaktiṃ nirupamām — O Mother, bestow on me in/toward you an incomparable devotion.

contentBlocks.original:
विधेर्मुण्डं हृत्वा यदकुरुत पात्रं करतले
हरिं शूलप्रोतं यदगमयदंसाभरणताम् ।
अलञ्चक्रे कण्ठं यदपि गरलेनाम्ब गिरिशः
शिवस्थायाः शक्तेस्तदिदमखिलं ते विलसितम् ॥ २२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidhērmuṇḍaṃ hṛtvā yadakuruta pātraṃ karatalē
hariṃ śūlaprōtaṃ yadagamayadaṃsābharaṇatām ।
alañchakrē kaṇṭhaṃ yadapi garalēnāmba giriśaḥ
śivasthāyāḥ śaktēstadidamakhilaṃ tē vilasitam ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: That [act by which] you made Brahmā's severed head into a begging-bowl in your hand; that [by which] you made Hari impaled on the trident into an ear-ornament; that [by which] Śiva adorned his throat with poison — O Mother, all of this is the playful display of your power that abides in Śiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: vidheḥ muṇḍaṃ hṛtvā — having taken Brahmā's (vidhē = Brahmā) head; yad akuruta pātraṃ karatale — whatever was made into a bowl in [your] hand; hariṃ śūla-prōtaṃ — Hari (Viṣṇu) impaled on the trident; yad agamayal aṃsa-ābharaṇatām — whatever was made to serve as a shoulder-ornament; alañchakrē kaṇṭhaṃ yad api garalēna amba giriśaḥ — O Mother, even that by which Śiva adorned his throat with poison; śiva-sthāyāḥ śaktēḥ tat idam akhilaṃ tē vilasitam — all of this is the playful display of your power abiding in Śiva.

contentBlocks.original:
विरिञ्च्याख्या मातः सृजसि हरिसञ्ज्ञा त्वमवसि
त्रिलोकीं रुद्राख्या हरसि विदधासीश्वरदशाम् ।
भवन्ती नादाख्या विहरसि च पाशौघदलनी
त्वमेवैकाऽनेका भवसि कृतिभेदैर्गिरिसुते ॥ २३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viriñchyākhyā mātaḥ sṛjasi harisañjñā tvamavasi
trilōkīṃ rudrākhyā harasi vidadhāsīśvaradaśām ।
bhavantī nādākhyā viharasi cha pāśaughadalanī
tvamēvaikā'nēkā bhavasi kṛtibhēdairgirisutē ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, named Viriñcī (Brahmā) you create; named Hari you sustain the three worlds; named Rudra you dissolve and establish the Īśvara-state. As Nāda you sport about, breaking all bonds. Though one alone, you become many through distinctions of action, O daughter of the mountain.
contentBlocks.meanings: viriñchī-ākhyā mātaḥ sṛjasi — named Viriñcī (Brahmā), O Mother, you create; hari-sañjñā tvam avasi trilōkīṃ — named Hari, you sustain the three worlds; rudrākhyā harasi — named Rudra, you dissolve; vidadhāsi īśvara-daśām — you establish the Īśvara-state; bhavantī nādākhyā viharasi — being/as Nāda you sport; pāśa-augha-dalanī — breaking/cutting the flood of bonds; tvam ēva ikā anēkā bhavasi — you alone, though one, become many; kṛti-bhēdaiḥ giri-sutē — through distinctions of action, O mountain-daughter.

contentBlocks.original:
मुनीनां चेतोभिः प्रमृदितकषायैरपि मना-
-गशक्यं संस्प्रष्टुं चकितचकितैरम्ब सततम् ।
श्रुतीनां मूर्धानः प्रकृतिकठिनाः कोमलतरे
कथं ते विन्दन्ते पदकिसलये पार्वति पदम् ॥ २४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
munīnāṃ chētōbhiḥ pramṛditakaṣāyairapi manā-
-gaśakyaṃ saṃspraṣṭuṃ chakitachakitairamba satatam ।
śrutīnāṃ mūrdhānaḥ prakṛtikaṭhināḥ kōmalatarē
kathaṃ tē vindantē padakisalayē pārvati padam ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Even by the minds of sages, purified of all impurities — your feet can barely be touched, ever only tremblingly. The summits of the Vedas (Upaniṣads), by their very nature hard as stone — O Pārvatī, how do they find their place on the tender buds of your feet?
contentBlocks.meanings: munīnāṃ chētōbhiḥ pramṛdita-kaṣāyaiḥ — by the minds of sages, their impurities thoroughly rubbed away; api manāk aśakyaṃ saṃspraṣṭuṃ — even barely possible to touch; chakita-chakitaiḥ amba — ever only tremblingly, O Mother; satatam — always; śrutīnāṃ mūrdhānaḥ — the summits/heads of the Vedas (Upaniṣads); prakṛti-kaṭhinā — naturally hard; kōmalatarē — on what is more tender; kathaṃ tē vindantē pada-kisalayē pārvati padam — how, O Pārvatī, do they find their place on the tender-bud feet?

contentBlocks.original:
तटिद्वल्लीं नित्याममृतसरितं पाररहितां
मलोत्तीर्णां ज्योत्स्नां प्रकृतिमगुणग्रन्थिगहनाम् ।
गिरां दूरां विद्यामविनतकुचां विश्वजननी-
-मपर्यन्तां लक्ष्मीमभिदधति सन्तो भगवतीम् ॥ २५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṭidvallīṃ nityāmamṛtasaritaṃ pārarahitāṃ
malōttīrṇāṃ jyōtsnāṃ prakṛtimaguṇagranthigahanām ।
girāṃ dūrāṃ vidyāmavinatakuchāṃ viśvajananī-
-maparyantāṃ lakṣmīmabhidadhati santō bhagavatīm ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Saints describe Bhagavatī as: a lightning-vine, the eternal one, the shoreless nectar-river, the one who has crossed all impurity, moonlight, nature unentangled in guṇa-knots, beyond speech, knowledge [personified], she whose bosom does not bow, Mother of the World, boundless, Lakṣmī.
contentBlocks.meanings: taṭit-vallīṃ — a lightning-vine; nityām — eternal; amṛta-saritaṃ — nectar-river; pāra-rahitāṃ — without a farther shore; mala-uttīrṇāṃ — one who has crossed impurity; jyōtsnāṃ — moonlight; prakṛtiṃ aguṇa-granthi-gahanām — nature that is deep/dense without guṇa-knots; girāṃ dūrāṃ — far beyond speech; vidyāṃ — knowledge [itself]; avinata-kuchāṃ — she whose breasts do not bow (to anyone); viśva-jananīm — Mother of the World; aparyantāṃ — boundless; lakṣmīm abhidadhati santaḥ bhagavatīm — the saints describe as Bhagavatī.

contentBlocks.original:
शरीरं क्षित्यम्भः प्रभृतिरचितं केवलमचित्
सुखं दुःखं चायं कलयति पुमांश्चेतन इति ।
स्फुटं जानानोऽपि प्रभवति न देही रहयितुं
शरीराहङ्कारं तव समयबाह्यो गिरिसुते ॥ २६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śarīraṃ kṣityambhaḥ prabhṛtirachitaṃ kēvalamachit
sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ chāyaṃ kalayati pumāṃśchētana iti ।
sphuṭaṃ jānānō'pi prabhavati na dēhī rahayituṃ
śarīrāhaṅkāraṃ tava samayabāhyō girisutē ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The body is pure inert matter — made of earth, water, and the rest; and this person, as a conscious one (cētana), experiences joy and sorrow. Though clearly knowing this, the embodied one cannot abandon the identification with the body — this is the one who is outside your tradition (samaya-bāhya), O daughter of the mountain.
contentBlocks.meanings: śarīraṃ kṣiti-ambhaḥ prabhṛti-rachitaṃ — the body, made of earth, water, and the rest; kēvalam achit — mere inert matter; sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ ca ayaṃ — and this one [experiences] joy and sorrow; kalayati pumāṃ cētanaḥ iti — the man considers [himself] conscious; sphuṭaṃ jānānaḥ api — though knowing this clearly; prabhavati na dēhī rahayituṃ — the embodied one is not able to abandon; śarīra-ahaṅkāraṃ — identification with the body; tava samaya-bāhyaḥ giri-sutē — one outside your samaya (tradition), O daughter of the mountain.

contentBlocks.original:
पिता माता भ्राता सुहृदनुचरः सद्म गृहिणी
वपुः क्षेत्रं मित्रं धनमपि यदा मां विजहति ।
तदा मे भिन्दाना सपदि भयमोहान्धतमसं
महाज्योत्स्ने मातर्भव करुणया सन्निधिकरी ॥ २७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pitā mātā bhrātā suhṛdanucharaḥ sadma gṛhiṇī
vapuḥ kṣētraṃ mitraṃ dhanamapi yadā māṃ vijahati ।
tadā mē bhindānā sapadi bhayamōhāndhatamasaṃ
mahājyōtsnē mātarbhava karuṇayā sannidhikarī ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: When father, mother, brother, dear friend, attendant, home, wife, body, land, friends, and wealth — when all these depart from me — then, O Mother of great moonlight, be swiftly present by your compassion, shattering the blinding darkness of fear and delusion.
contentBlocks.meanings: pitā mātā bhrātā — father, mother, brother; suhṛd-anucharaḥ — dear friend and attendant; sadma gṛhiṇī — home and wife; vapuḥ kṣētraṃ mitraṃ dhanam api — body, land, friends, and even wealth; yadā māṃ vijahati — when these all leave me; tadā mē bhindānā — then breaking [for me]; sapadi — swiftly; bhaya-mōha-andha-tamasaṃ — the blindly dark [cloud] of fear and delusion; mahā-jyōtsnē mātaḥ — O Mother of great moonlight; bhava karuṇayā sannidhikarī — be present/near by your compassion.

contentBlocks.original:
सुता दक्षस्यादौ किल सकलमातस्त्वमुदभूः
सदोषं तं हित्वा तदनु गिरिराजस्य दुहिता ।
अनाद्यन्ता शम्भोरपृथगपि शक्तिर्भगवती
विवाहाज्जायासीत्यहह चरितं वेत्ति तव कः ॥ २८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sutā dakṣasyādau kila sakalamātastvamudabhūḥ
sadōṣaṃ taṃ hitvā tadanu girirājasya duhitā ।
anādyantā śambhōrapṛthagapi śaktirbhagavatī
vivāhājjāyāsītyahaha charitaṃ vētti tava kaḥ ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother of all, you first arose as Satī, daughter of Dakṣa — then left that form with its fault — and thereafter were born as the daughter of the mountain-king. Eternally non-beginning, non-ending, inseparable power of Śambhu — yet you became his wife through marriage! Ah, who can know your story?
contentBlocks.meanings: sutā dakṣasya ādau — first as the daughter of Dakṣa; kila sakala-mātar tvam udabhūḥ — you indeed arose, O Mother of all; sadōṣaṃ taṃ hitvā — having left that [form/body] with its fault; tadanu giri-rājasya duhitā — then the daughter of the mountain-king (Himavān); anādyantā — without beginning or end; śambhoḥ apṛthag api śaktiḥ bhagavatī — even inseparable śakti of Śambhu, Bhagavatī; vivāhāt jāyā asīt — you became [his] wife through marriage; aha aha charitaṃ vētti tava kaḥ — O how wondrous, who knows your story?

contentBlocks.original:
कणास्त्वद्दीप्तीनां रविशशिकृशानुप्रभृतयः
परं ब्रह्म क्षुद्रं तव नियतमानन्दकणिका ।
शिवादि क्षित्यन्तं त्रिवलयतनोः सर्वमुदरे
तवास्ते भक्तस्य स्फुरसि हृदि चित्रं भगवति ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṇāstvaddīptīnāṃ raviśaśikṛśānuprabhṛtayaḥ
paraṃ brahma kṣudraṃ tava niyatamānandakaṇikā ।
śivādi kṣityantaṃ trivalayatanōḥ sarvamudarē
tavāstē bhaktasya sphurasi hṛdi chitraṃ bhagavati ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The sun, moon, fire, and the rest — mere sparks of your radiance. Para Brahman — merely a tiny particle of your bliss. All from Śiva down to the earth, within the body of the three-fold coil — all dwells in your womb. Yet, O wondrous Bhagavatī, you flash within the heart of your devotee.
contentBlocks.meanings: kaṇāḥ tvad-dīptīnāṃ — mere sparks of your radiance; ravi-śaśi-kṛśānu-prabhṛtayaḥ — the sun, moon, fire, and the rest; paraṃ brahma — Para Brahman (the supreme Absolute); kṣudraṃ — small/tiny; tava niyatam ānanda-kaṇikā — certainly a tiny particle of your bliss; śivādi kṣiti-antaṃ — from Śiva down to earth; tri-valaya-tanoḥ — of the three-fold body (the three coils of the serpent/cosmos); sarvam udarē tavāstē — all dwells in your womb; bhaktasya sphurasi hṛdi — you flash/appear in the devotee's heart; chitraṃ bhagavati — O Bhagavatī, how wondrous.

contentBlocks.original:
पुरः पश्चादन्तर्बहिरपरिमेयं परिमितं
परं स्थूलं सूक्ष्मं सकलमकुलं गुह्यमगुहम् ।
दवीयो नेदीयः सदसदिति विश्वं भगवती
सदा पश्यन्त्याख्यां वहसि भुवनक्षोभजननीम् ॥ ३० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puraḥ paśchādantarbahiraparimēyaṃ parimitaṃ
paraṃ sthūlaṃ sūkṣmaṃ sakalamakulaṃ guhyamaguham ।
davīyō nēdīyaḥ sadasaditi viśvaṃ bhagavatī
sadā paśyantyākhyāṃ vahasi bhuvanakṣōbhajananīm ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Before, behind, within, without — immeasurable and measured, supreme and gross, subtle, sakala (with parts) and akula (partless), hidden and manifest, very far and very near, sat (being) and asat (non-being) — thus you bear the name Paśyantī (the Seeing One), O Bhagavatī, Mother of all universe-perturbations.
contentBlocks.meanings: puraḥ paśchāt antaḥ bahiḥ — before, behind, within, without; aparimēyaṃ parimitaṃ — immeasurable and measured; paraṃ sthūlaṃ sūkṣmaṃ — supreme, gross, subtle; sakalam akulaṃ — with parts (sakala) and partless (akula); guhyam aguham — hidden and unhidden/manifest; davīyaḥ nēdīyaḥ — very far and very near; sadasat iti viśvaṃ — the whole world as sat and asat; bhagavatī sadā paśyantyākhyāṃ vahasi — O Bhagavatī, you always bear the name Paśyantī; bhuvana-kṣōbha-jananīm — O Mother of all universe-perturbations.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रविश्य त्वन्मार्गं सहजदयया देशिकदृशा
षडध्वध्वान्तौघच्छिदुरगणनातीतकरुणाम् ।
परामाज्ञाकारां सपदि शिवयन्तीं शिवतनुं
स्वमात्मानं धन्याश्चिरमुपलभन्ते भगवतीम् ॥ ३१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praviśya tvanmārgaṃ sahajadayayā dēśikadṛśā
ṣaḍadhvadhvāntaughachChiduragaṇanātītakaruṇām ।
parāmājñākārāṃ sapadi śivayantīṃ śivatanuṃ
svamātmānaṃ dhanyāśchiramupalabhantē bhagavatīm ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Entering your path — through the eyes of the guru, endowed with natural compassion that destroys the dense darkness of the six paths, beyond counting — the blessed ones attain for long [the vision of] you, Bhagavatī: she who takes the form of the supreme Ājñā, who swiftly renders the devotee's Śiva-body [pure and divine].
contentBlocks.meanings: praviśya tvan-mārgaṃ — having entered your path; sahaja-dayayā — through natural/innate compassion; dēśika-dṛśā — through the eyes of the guru (dēśika); ṣaḍ-adhva-dhvāntaugha-chidiura-gaṇanā-atīta-karuṇāṃ — compassion that destroys the dense darkness of the six paths (ṣaḍadhvā), beyond counting; parām ājñā-kārāṃ — one who takes the form of the supreme Ājñā (command/sixth chakra); sapadi śivayantīṃ śiva-tanuṃ — swiftly making the Śiva-body [pure]; svam ātmānaṃ dhanyāḥ chiram upalabhantē bhagavatīm — the blessed ones attain you, Bhagavatī, for long as their own Self.

contentBlocks.original:
मयूखाः पूष्णीव ज्वलन इव तद्दीप्तिकणिकाः
पयोधौ कल्लोलाः प्रतिहतमहिम्नीव पृषतः ।
उदेत्योदेत्याम्ब त्वयि सह निजैः सात्त्विकगुणै-
-र्भजन्ते तत्त्वौघाः प्रशममनुकल्पं परवशाः ॥ ३२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mayūkhāḥ pūṣṇīva jvalana iva taddīptikaṇikāḥ
payōdhau kallōlāḥ pratihatamahimnīva pṛṣataḥ ।
udētyōdētyāmba tvayi saha nijaiḥ sāttvikaguṇai-
-rbhajantē tattvaughāḥ praśamamanukalpaṃ paravaśāḥ ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: As the sun's rays [into the sun], as sparks [into fire's brightness], as ocean waves [into the ocean], as drops [scattered from arrested greatness] — O Mother, the hosts of tattvas (cosmic principles), ever rising and falling with their own sāttvika qualities, helplessly under your dominion, dissolve into you for an era and then emerge again.
contentBlocks.meanings: mayūkhāḥ pūṣṇī iva — as rays [return] to the sun; jvalana iva tad-dīpti-kaṇikāḥ — as sparks of fire's brightness [return to fire]; payōdhau kallōlāḥ — as waves in the ocean [return to the ocean]; pratihat-mahimnī iva pṛṣataḥ — as drops [scattered] from arrested greatness; udēty udēty amba — ever rising, rising again, O Mother; tvayi saha nijaiḥ sāttvika-guṇaiḥ — into you, with their own sāttvika qualities; bhajantē tattvaughāḥ — the hosts of tattvas attain; praśamam anu-kalpaṃ — dissolution for an era; paravaśāḥ — helplessly under [your] dominion.

contentBlocks.original:
विधुर्विष्णुर्ब्रह्मा प्रकृतिरणुरात्मा दिनकरः
स्वभावो जैनेन्द्रः सुगतमुनिराकाशमलिनः ।
शिवः शक्तिश्चेति श्रुतिविषयतां तामुपगतां
विकल्पैरेभिस्त्वामभिदधति सन्तो भगवतीम् ॥ ३३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidhurviṣṇurbrahmā prakṛtiraṇurātmā dinakaraḥ
svabhāvō jainēndraḥ sugatamunirākāśamalinaḥ ।
śivaḥ śaktiśchēti śrutiviṣayatāṃ tāmupagatāṃ
vikalpairēbhistvāmabhidadhati santō bhagavatīm ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The moon, Viṣṇu, Brahmā, Prakṛti, the atom, the soul, the sun, self-nature (svabhāva), the Jina (Jain principle), the Buddhist sage, the stained-space (ākāśa-mala), Śiva, Śakti — the saints declare you, Bhagavatī, as having taken on the form accessible to scripture through all these diverse conceptions.
contentBlocks.meanings: vidhuḥ viṣṇuḥ brahmā — moon, Viṣṇu, Brahmā; prakṛtiḥ aṇuḥ ātmā dinakaraḥ — Prakṛti, atom, soul, sun; svabhāvaḥ — self-nature (Buddhist svabhāva-vāda); jainēndraḥ — the Jain principle; sugata-muniḥ — the Buddha (sugata = gone to bliss); ākāśa-alinaḥ — sky/space [with its impurity] (ākāśa-malin — perhaps the Vaiśeṣika concept of ākāśa); śivaḥ śaktiḥ ca iti — Śiva and Śakti; śruti-viṣayatāṃ tām upagatāṃ — having taken on the form accessible to scriptural texts; vikalpaiḥ ēbhiḥ tvām abhidadhati santaḥ bhagavatīm — through these concepts the saints declare you as Bhagavatī.

contentBlocks.original:
शिवस्त्वं शक्तिस्त्वं त्वमसि समया त्वं समयिनी
त्वमात्मा त्वं दीक्षा त्वमयमणिमादिर्गुणगणः ।
अविद्या त्वं विद्या त्वमसि निखिलं त्वं किमपरं
पृथक्तत्त्वं त्वत्तो भगवति न वीक्षामह इमे ॥ ३४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivastvaṃ śaktistvaṃ tvamasi samayā tvaṃ samayinī
tvamātmā tvaṃ dīkṣā tvamayamaṇimādirguṇagaṇaḥ ।
avidyā tvaṃ vidyā tvamasi nikhilaṃ tvaṃ kimaparaṃ
pṛthaktattvaṃ tvattō bhagavati na vīkṣāmaha imē ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śiva is you; Śakti is you; you are Samayā (the esoteric unity); you are Samayinī (the adept of Samaya); you are the Self; you are Dīkṣā (initiation); you are Aṇimā-ādi (the siddhis beginning with anima); you are Avidyā (ignorance); you are Vidyā (knowledge); you are everything. O Bhagavatī, we see no separate tattva [other than you] — distinct from you.
contentBlocks.meanings: śivaḥ tvaṃ — Śiva is you; śaktiḥ tvaṃ — Śakti is you; tvam asi samayā — you are Samayā (the Śrī Vidyā tradition's principle of union); tvaṃ samayinī — you are Samayinī (the practitioner of Samaya); tvam ātmā — you are the Self; tvaṃ dīkṣā — you are Dīkṣā; tvam ayam aṇimā-ādiḥ guṇa-gaṇaḥ — you are these, Aṇimā and the other siddhis; avidyā tvaṃ — you are Avidyā (ignorance); vidyā tvam asi — you are Vidyā; nikhilaṃ tvaṃ — you are everything; kim aparaṃ — what else [is there]; pṛthak tattvaṃ tvattaḥ bhagavati na vīkṣāmahe imē — O Bhagavatī, we see no tattva separate from you.

contentBlocks.original:
त्वयासौ जानीते रचयति भवत्यैव सततं
त्वयैवेच्छत्यम्ब त्वमसि निखिला यस्य तनवः ।
जगत्साम्यं शम्भोर्वहसि परमव्योमवपुषः
तथाप्यर्धं भूत्वा विहरसि शिवस्येति किमिदम् ॥ ३५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvayāsau jānītē rachayati bhavatyaiva satataṃ
tvayaivēchChatyamba tvamasi nikhilā yasya tanavaḥ ।
jagatsāmyaṃ śambhōrvahasi paramavyōmavapuṣaḥ
tathāpyardhaṃ bhūtvā viharasi śivasyēti kimidam ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Through you alone that Being knows; through you alone he ever creates; through you alone he wills, O Mother; you are all of whose bodies are all things. You carry the universal nature equal to Śambhu of the supreme-ether body — yet you sport as his half (ardhāṅginī)! What wonder is this?
contentBlocks.meanings: tvayā asau jānītē — through you alone that one knows; rachayati bhavatyai eva satatam — creates only by/through you, always; tvayaiva ichchati amba — through you alone he wills, O Mother; tvam asi nikhilā yasya tanavaḥ — you are all of whose bodies are all things; jagat-sāmyaṃ śambhoḥ vahasi — you carry universal nature equal to Śambhu; parama-vyōma-vapuṣaḥ — of Śambhu whose body is the supreme ether; tathā api ardhaṃ bhūtvā viharasi śivasya iti kim idam — yet even so, becoming his half, you sport as Śiva's — what wonder is this?

contentBlocks.original:
असङ्ख्यैः प्राचीनैर्जननि जननैः कर्मविलया-
-त्सकृज्जन्मन्यन्ते गुरुवपुषमासाद्य गिरिशम् ।
अवाप्याज्ञां शैवीं शिवतनुमपि त्वां विदितवा-
-न्नयेयं त्वत्पूजास्तुतिविरचनेनैव दिवसान् ॥ ३६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
asaṅkhyaiḥ prāchīnairjanani jananaiḥ karmavilayā-
-tsakṛjjanmanyantē guruvapuṣamāsādya giriśam ।
avāpyājñāṃ śaivīṃ śivatanumapi tvāṃ viditavā-
-nnayēyaṃ tvatpūjāstutivirachanēnaiva divasān ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, through countless previous births — through the gradual exhaustion of karma accumulated in those births — attaining at last in one birth the guru who has the form of Śiva, having received the Śaiva initiation, having known you and your Śiva-body — may I spend my days solely in worshipping, praising, and composing [verse] for you.
contentBlocks.meanings: asaṅkhyaiḥ prāchīnaiḥ janmabhiḥ — through countless previous births; karma-vilayāt — through the exhaustion/dissolution of karma; sakṛt janmani antē — at the end, in one birth; guru-vapuṣam āsādya giriśam — having attained the guru who has Śiva's form; avāpya ājñāṃ śaivīṃ — having received the Śaiva initiation/command; śiva-tanum api tvāṃ viditavān — having known you, the Śiva-body; nayēyaṃ tvatpūjā-stuti-virachanēna eva divasān — may I spend my days solely in your worship, praise, and composition.

contentBlocks.original:
यत्षट्पत्रं कमलमुदितं तस्य या कर्णिकाख्या
योनिस्तस्याः प्रथितमुदरे यत्तदोङ्कारपीठम् ।
तस्याप्यन्तः कुचभरनतां कुण्डलीति प्रसिद्धां
श्यामाकारां सकलजननीं सन्ततं भावयामि ॥ ३७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yatṣaṭpatraṃ kamalamuditaṃ tasya yā karṇikākhyā
yōnistasyāḥ prathitamudarē yattadōṅkārapīṭham ।
tasyāpyantaḥ kuchabharanatāṃ kuṇḍalīti prasiddhāṃ
śyāmākārāṃ sakalajananīṃ santataṃ bhāvayāmi ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the six-petaled lotus that has arisen, the pericarp (karṇikā) is the sacred yoni; in the spread of that yoni, the well-known Oṃkāra-throne. Within that too — bowing under the weight of the bosom, known as Kuṇḍalinī — that dark-hued Sakala-Jananī I ever contemplate.
contentBlocks.meanings: yat ṣaṭ-patraṃ kamalam uditaṃ — the six-petaled lotus that has arisen [in meditation]; tasya yā karṇikā-ākhyā — its pericarp known as karṇikā; yoniḥ — the sacred womb/yoni; tasyāḥ prathitam udarē — in the spread of that; yat tad oṅkāra-pīṭham — that which is the Oṃkāra-throne; tasyāpi antaḥ — within that too; kucha-bhara-natāṃ — bowing under the weight of the bosom; kuṇḍalī iti prasiddhāṃ — known as Kuṇḍalinī; śyāmā-kārāṃ sakala-jananīṃ santataṃ bhāvayāmi — I ever contemplate the dark-hued Sakala-Jananī.

contentBlocks.original:
भुवि पयसि कृशानौ मारुते खे शशाङ्के
सवितरि यजमानेऽप्यष्टधा शक्तिरेका ।
वहसि कुचभराभ्यां यावनम्रापि विश्वं
सकलजननि सा त्वं पाहि मामित्यवाच्यम् ॥ ३८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhuvi payasi kṛśānau mārutē khē śaśāṅkē
savitari yajamānē'pyaṣṭadhā śaktirēkā ।
vahasi kuchabharābhyāṃ yāvanamrāpi viśvaṃ
sakalajanani sā tvaṃ pāhi māmityavāchyam ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In earth, in water, in fire, in wind, in sky, in the moon, in the sun, and in the worshipper too — as one Śakti manifesting eightfold. Though you bow the world under the weight of your twin breasts, O Sakala-Jananī — you are That [one power]. Protect me — this [prayer] is beyond words.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhuvi payasi kṛśānau mārutē khē śaśāṅkē savitari yajamānē api — in earth, water, fire, wind, sky, moon, sun, and even the worshipper/sacrificer; aṣṭadhā śaktiḥ ēkā — one Śakti manifesting eightfold; vahasi kuchabhara-ābhyāṃ yāv anamrā api viśvaṃ — you, though bowing the world under the weight of the twin breasts; sakala-janani sā tvaṃ — O Sakala-Jananī, that [one] is you; pāhi mām ity avāchyam — protect me — this [petition] is beyond speech.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीकालिदास विरचित पञ्चस्तव्यां पञ्चमः सकलजननीस्तवः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīkāḻidāsa virachita pañchastavyāṃ pañchamaḥ sakalajananīstavaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus [ends] the fifth Sakala-Jananī-Stava of the Pañchastavya composed by Śrī Kālidāsa.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti — thus; śrī kālidāsa virachita — composed by Śrī Kālidāsa; pañchastavyāṃ — in the [set of] five stavyas (the Pañchastavya); pañchamaḥ — the fifth; sakala-jananī-stavaḥ — the Sakala-Jananī-Stava.
